questions.question: What does `pub` do in Rust?
options: Makes an item accessible outside its module
questions.explanation: `pub` makes items public. Without it, items are private to their defining module.

questions.question: Module contents are private by default.
questions.explanation: Rust defaults to private. You must add `pub` to expose items outside the module.

questions.question: Bring a function into scope from another module:
questions.explanation: `use` creates a shortcut so you don't need the full path every time.

questions.question: What is the crate root for a binary project?
questions.explanation: `src/main.rs` is the crate root for binaries; `src/lib.rs` for libraries.

questions.question: Make this function accessible outside the module:
questions.explanation: `pub` makes the function visible to code outside the `math` module.

questions.question: Making a struct `pub` automatically makes all its fields public.
questions.explanation: A pub struct still has private fields by default. Each field needs its own `pub` keyword.
